Title: LpNAC6 reversely regulates the alkali tolerance and drought tolerance of Lilium pumilum

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Functional characterization of LpNAC6 is performed in Nicotiana tabacum as a heterologous system; its native function in Lilium pumilum is inferred but not directly tested here. The authors note that opposite effects of LpNAC6 on alkali and drought tolerance may be influenced by insertion sites of the transgene in the tobacco genome, and that precise insertion sites and potential effects on other tobacco genes remain to be determined.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression of LpNAC6 in tobacco enhances alkali tolerance but reduces drought tolerance at seed, seedling, and adult stages, as evidenced by better growth, higher survival, and improved physiological status under alkali stress, and conversely poorer performance and survival under drought.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: LpNAC6 alters antioxidant defense (upregulating SOD, POD, CAT activities and lowering H2O2, O2^- and MDA under alkali stress but doing the opposite under drought), modulates osmotic adjustment (higher proline under alkali, lower under drought), maintains chlorophyll content and photosynthetic capacity under alkali but accelerates their decline under drought, and changes expression of K+ transporter NtHAK1, lipid transporter NtLTP1, and LEA genes NtLEA5 and NtERD10C, thereby exerting opposite effects on alkali versus drought tolerance.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Abiotic stress-responsive transcriptional regulatory pathway involving modulation of ROS-scavenging, ion homeostasis, osmotic stress responses, and LEA-mediated dehydration tolerance under alkali and drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: In LpNAC6-overexpressing tobacco under alkali stress, LpNAC6 positively regulates expression of stress-related genes NtSOD, NtPOD, NtCAT, NtHAK1, and NtLTP1 (LpNAC6 -->(activation under alkali stress)--> NtSOD/NtPOD/NtCAT/NtHAK1/NtLTP1), enhancing antioxidant capacity and ion/membrane protection. Under drought stress, LpNAC6 negatively regulates expression of NtSOD, NtPOD, NtCAT, NtLEA5, and NtERD10C (LpNAC6 --| (negative regulation under drought stress)--> NtSOD/NtPOD/NtCAT/NtLEA5/NtERD10C), reducing ROS-scavenging and LEA-mediated osmoprotection.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: LpNAC6 is highly expressed in three independent transgenic tobacco lines (Tr6-1, Tr6-2, Tr6-3) compared with WT, as confirmed by qRT-PCR; lines with relatively high LpNAC6 expression in T1 were selected to generate T2 seeds used for stress assays.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Alkali stress imposed with NaHCO3: 5–15 mM in 1/2 MS medium for seeds and seedlings and 300 mM NaHCO3 irrigation (100 mL per plant every other day) in soil; drought stress imposed by mannitol (100–300 mM) in 1/2 MS medium and by withholding water for 30 days in soil followed by 7 days of rewatering; all at 23 ± 2 °C, 16 h light/8 h dark.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response; alkali tolerance; drought tolerance; stress physiology; transcriptional regulation by NAC factor.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (alkali stress tolerance; drought stress tolerance).
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Under alkali stress, plant height of Tr6-1, Tr6-2, and Tr6-3 was 1.25, 1.23, and 1.27 times that of WT after 10 days, and 1.45, 1.43, and 1.43 times that of WT after 15 days (300 mM NaHCO3). Under drought stress, WT plant height was 1.26, 1.20, and 1.31 times that of Tr6-1, Tr6-2, and Tr6-3 after 20 days; post-rewatering survival was 66.67% in WT vs 22.22%, 38.89%, and 19.44% in Tr6-1, Tr6-2, and Tr6-3. Under mannitol-induced drought at 150–200 mM, WT seedling root length was 1.31–1.48× and fresh weight 1.24–1.67× that of transgenic lines.
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: LpNAC6-overexpressing plants show slower recovery after drought, with lower post-rewatering survival, reduced plant height, fresh weight, and root length compared with WT; LpNAC6 negatively affects recovery from dehydration stress.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: LpNAC6-overexpressing Nicotiana tabacum (tobacco) lines Tr6-1, Tr6-2, Tr6-3 (T2 generation) and wild-type tobacco plants.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Generation of LpNAC6-overexpressing tobacco (selection of T2 seeds from highly expressing T1 lines); qRT-PCR analysis of LpNAC6 and stress-responsive genes (NtSOD, NtPOD, NtCAT, NtHAK1, NtLTP1, NtLEA5, NtERD10C) using Ct 2^-ΔΔCt method; alkali stress treatments with NaHCO3 (5–15 mM in 1/2 MS; 300 mM in soil) and drought stress using mannitol (100–300 mM) in 1/2 MS and 30-day water withholding followed by rewatering in soil; measurement of seed germination, seedling root length and fresh weight, plant height, survival, and root growth; biochemical assays of SOD, POD, CAT activities; quantification of H2O2, O2^-, MDA, proline, and chlorophyll contents; gas-exchange measurements of photosynthetic parameters (Pn, Tr, Gs, Ci).
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation by overexpression of LpNAC6 in tobacco followed by controlled alkali and drought stress assays, combined with physiological/biochemical measurements and qRT-PCR analysis of stress-related genes.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Previously isolated as a salt-stress-responsive NAC gene from Lilium pumilum and cloned; function analyzed by overexpressing LpNAC6 in transgenic tobacco lines and subjecting them to alkali and drought stress.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: LpNAC6 is a candidate gene for improving alkali tolerance of Lilium pumilum and potentially other crops grown on arid saline-alkali soils, as its overexpression markedly enhances alkali tolerance in a heterologous system. However, because it simultaneously decreases drought tolerance and post-drought recovery, its use in molecular breeding requires balancing alkali and drought environments and careful deployment in breeding programs for resistant new varieties of Lilium pumilum in arid saline-alkali regions.
